text: The patient is positioned in the supine and Trendelenburg position for robotic surgery. Conventional robotic trocars are placed. The surgeon begins by freeing pelvic adhesions and instills fluid through the urethral catheter to identify the bladder. Dissection of the left ureter is performed at its distal and proximal portions, revealing a short stenosis at the left ureteroenteric anastomosis.

The surgeon performs direct ureteral reimplantation using an exteriorized ureteral catheter. The right ureter is freed and dissected, revealing a stenosis. The ureter is confirmed to reach the bladder with tension.
The surgeon creates a new bladder flap and performs a body-type ureteral reimplantation.
